skill.name: broken-authentication
skill.description: Guides authorized assessment of broken authentication, session management, password reset, rate limiting, and MFA weaknesses in web applications.
skill.summary: Assess authentication and session management controls for authorized web application security testing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command execution and network findings are mostly Markdown examples, not executable package code, so those scanner hits are false positives for embedded malware. The skill is still high risk because it gives actionable brute force, credential stuffing, MFA bypass, lockout bypass, and account takeover testing workflows, including evasion tactics. No prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

high_findings.title: Actionable Brute Force and Credential Stuffing Guidance
high_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run password guessing and credential stuffing tests, including Hydra usage, breached credential lists, and detection evasion tactics such as rotating source IPs and randomizing user agents. This is dual-use content that can enable unauthorized account compromise if used outside a written test scope.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections explicitly describe brute force tooling, breached credential inputs, and evasion behavior. The skill includes authorization language, so malicious intent is not confirmed, but the abuse potential is clear.

high_findings.title: Authentication Bypass and Token Tampering Playbooks
high_findings.description: The skill provides step-by-step testing for OTP bypass, direct MFA step skipping, response manipulation, JWT none algorithm abuse, and submission of modified authorization tokens. These workflows are actionable exploitation guidance for account privilege escalation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context confirms exploitation workflows, not just abstract vulnerability descriptions. The examples are framed as testing, which keeps this below confirmed malicious intent.

high_findings.title: Account Takeover Workflow Examples
high_findings.description: The skill describes session fixation, password reset token manipulation, host header injection in reset flows, and changing reset parameters toward another account. These examples can guide unauthorized access if copied against real services.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited material gives concrete account takeover scenarios and parameter manipulation steps. Authorization constraints are present elsewhere, so this is high risk rather than confirmed malicious.

medium_findings.title: Production Network Interaction Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes examples that send HTTP requests to target applications and collect session cookies. These are expected for security testing, but they require explicit scope controls and could affect real systems if used carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is documented in examples and appears intended for authorized assessment. Risk remains because the examples interact with authentication endpoints and session state.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Detected as Ruby Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many fenced Markdown blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation and no executable Ruby file or package script is present, so these detections are false positives for embedded command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns appear inside Markdown code fences or prose examples. No evidence of executable Ruby backtick calls was found in the only scanned file.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs and IP Headers Detected as Network Indicators
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs and IP addresses are placeholder target examples and HTTP header examples, not evidence of calls to attacker-controlled infrastructure. They still indicate that the skill teaches network-facing authentication tests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use generic target.com examples and the IP addresses are local or private header values. This supports a false positive for exfiltration or command-and-control infrastructure.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword Matches Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Static matches for weak cryptography and C2 keywords appear in authentication testing context, such as JWT algorithm testing and bearer token examples. No evidence found of malware, command-and-control behavior, or cryptographic implementation code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are documentation examples for authentication weaknesses. The file contains no runnable cryptographic library implementation or C2 client.

dangerous_patterns.title: Detection Evasion in Credential Attacks
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill advises slow request rates, source IP rotation, user-agent randomization, and delays during credential stuffing. These tactics increase misuse risk and should not be included in publishable marketplace content without strict gating.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evasion guidance is explicit and directly tied to credential stuffing. Legitimate testing use is possible, but marketplace publication risk is high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Rate Limit Bypass Header Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill lists spoofable IP headers and demonstrates changing X-Forwarded-For values during login attempts. This can help bypass rate limiting and account lockout controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The content explicitly links spoofed headers to lockout bypass testing. This is a recognized abuse pattern for authentication attacks.

content.value_statement: Authentication flaws can expose accounts, sessions, and password reset flows to takeover. This skill guides structured, authorized testing and clear reporting for web application authentication controls.
actual_capabilities: Maps login, registration, reset, logout, API auth, and OAuth-related endpoints.
Evaluates password policy enforcement, account lockout, and rate limiting behavior.
Reviews session cookie attributes, session rotation, idle timeout, and absolute timeout controls.
Assesses MFA enrollment, OTP verification, backup recovery, and bypass-resistant workflow design.
Documents authentication weaknesses with remediation guidance for authorized security reports.
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization and scoped test accounts before use.
Does not provide safe automated controls to prevent impact on production systems.
May be blocked by CAPTCHA, rate limits, MFA protections, or third-party identity providers.
Includes high-risk offensive techniques that are not suitable for unsupervised marketplace publishing.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Authentication Review
use_cases.description: Check login, password reset, session, and MFA workflows before a web application release.

use_cases.title: Penetration Test Planning
use_cases.description: Create an authorized test plan for authentication controls, lockout behavior, and session handling.

use_cases.title: Security Remediation Backlog
use_cases.description: Translate authentication assessment results into prioritized fixes for product and engineering teams.

prompt_templates.title: Review Auth Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a safe scope for an authorized broken authentication assessment. Include required permissions, test accounts, endpoints, and out-of-scope actions.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Session Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this session management design for weaknesses. Focus on cookie flags, session rotation after login, logout invalidation, idle timeout, and absolute timeout.

prompt_templates.title: Build Test Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an authorized authentication testing checklist for login, MFA, password reset, account lockout, and API authentication. Include evidence to collect and expected secure behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these authentication test notes into prioritized findings with impact, reproduction summary, affected controls, risk rating, and remediation guidance.

output_examples.input: Assess the password reset workflow for a scoped test application.
output_examples.output: A report section covering reset token lifetime, single-use enforcement, account binding, host validation, and recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: Review session management findings from a web application test.
output: Session cookies should use HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite attributes.
Sessions should rotate after authentication and be invalidated after logout.
Idle and absolute timeout controls should match the application risk profile.

output_examples.input: Prepare a remediation plan for weak authentication controls.
output_examples.output: A prioritized action plan for stronger password policy, rate limiting, MFA hardening, session rotation, and monitoring.

best_practices: Confirm written authorization, scope, accounts, timing, and rate limits before any test.
Use dedicated test accounts and avoid real breached credentials or real user accounts.
Report impact, evidence, and remediation steps without exposing reusable secrets or active tokens.
anti_patterns: Do not test authentication controls on systems without explicit permission.
Do not run high-volume password guessing against production accounts.
Do not include live credentials, session tokens, or reset links in shared reports.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for public marketplace publication?
faq.answer: No. It contains high-risk dual-use authentication attack workflows and evasion guidance.

faq.question: Does the skill contain executable malware?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The only scanned file is Markdown documentation with examples.

faq.question: What authorization is required?
faq.answer: Use requires written permission, scoped targets, approved timing, and dedicated test accounts.

faq.question: Can this help with defensive reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure reviews of password policy, sessions, MFA, lockout, and reset flows.

faq.question: What are the main risks?
faq.answer: The main risks are credential attacks, account takeover testing, bypass guidance, and misuse against real users.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
